name: Step
comment:
Some stuff

@docs Step, map, noop, orElse, run, to, withCmd, mapMsg, exit, mapExit, onExit, asUpdateFunction

unions.name: Step
unions.comment:
Step

a `Step state msg output` describes one step of a state machine.

On each step, the state machine can either

  - `noop`, staying in the same state and executing no commands
  - step `to` some new `state`, potentially executing `msg`-returning commands
  - `exit`, and return a value of type `output`

values.name: asUpdateFunction
values.comment:
asUpdateFunction

a little helper function for the common case: turn an update function that returns a `Step` to a normal elm architecture update function
uses `run` internally to default with the provided model in case of a `noop`
values.type: (msg -> model -> Step model msg Never) -> msg -> model -> ( model, Cmd msg )

values.name: map
values.type: (a -> b) -> Step a msg output -> Step b msg output

values.name: mapExit
values.type: (o -> p) -> Step state msg o -> Step state msg p

values.name: mapMsg
values.type: (a -> b) -> Step state a output -> Step state b output

values.name: onExit
values.type: (o -> Step state msg p) -> Step state msg o -> Step state msg p

values.name: orElse
values.type: Step state msg output -> Step state msg output -> Step state msg output

values.name: run
values.type: Step state msg Never -> Maybe ( state, Cmd msg )

values.name: to
values.comment: step to a state with no commands

values.name: withCmd
values.type: Cmd msg -> Step state msg output -> Step state msg output
